validation_report.story_title: Update Story Template to v2.2 with depends_on Field

deferred_items.description: Story-Creation Skill Phase 1 Dependency Question - asks optional dependency question during story creation
deferred_items.deferral_reason: Requires modifications to devforgeai-story-creation skill Phase 1 workflow - scoped beyond core template updates

deferred_items.description: Input Normalization Logic - parse user dependency input into array format (handles 'none', 'STORY-044', 'STORY-044, STORY-045')
deferred_items.deferral_reason: Requires skill integration testing and Phase 1 workflow enhancement - deferred to separate story

details: Both deferrals clearly articulate scope boundary (skill enhancement vs template updates)
Rationale explains why work is deferred (integration requirements, skill testing)
No 'will add later' or vague justifications - specificity is clear

technical_blocker_assessment.blocker_description: devforgeai-story-creation skill requires Phase 1 enhancement and integration testing
analysis: AC#5 requires modifications to skill Phase 1 workflow - NOT internal template logic
Normalizing input requires testing skill integration - separate story justified
Current story completes 5 of 7 ACs without this blocker - strong standalone value
Blocker is external framework component (skill), not internal code decision
technical_blocker_assessment.resolution_path: STORY-091 (Git Worktree Auto-Management) depends_on STORY-090 and is scoped for EPIC-010, suggests future story-creation enhancement will follow
technical_blocker_assessment.recommendation: Blocker is VALID and properly justified

analysis: Could AC#5 be implemented in current story? Technically yes (add Phase 1 question to skill)
Should it be? No - requires testing STORY-091 integration and Phase 1 workflow validation
Current story provides 5 complete ACs: template file update, format version increment, changelog, story standardization (6 stories), sync
These 5 ACs deliver standalone value (dependency tracking foundation for EPIC-010)
AC#5 is enhancement to story-creation skill - separate story appropriate
implementation_feasibility.value_without_ac5: Very High - template provides depends_on infrastructure for entire EPIC-010
implementation_feasibility.recommendation: Deferral is appropriate - AC#5 is properly scoped as skill enhancement

adr_requirement_check.original_scope: AC#5 is in Definition of Done (was in scope)
adr_requirement_check.scope_change_type: Moving AC#5 from current story to follow-up story
adr_requirement_check.business_impact: Changes feature delivery timing - AC#5 moves from STORY-090 to future story
adr_requirement_check.adr_required_because: Deferrals in-scope DoD items requires architectural documentation per critical-rules.md
adr_requirement_check.adr_recommendation: Create ADR documenting why AC#5 (skill enhancement) scoped separately from AC#1-4 (template updates)
adr_requirement_check.suggested_adr_title: ADR-008: Defer Story-Creation Skill Enhancement to Separate Story (AC#5 Scope Boundary)

suggested_adr_content.context: STORY-090 includes AC#5 (skill Phase 1 enhancement) in original DoD. During development, determined skill modifications and integration testing should be separate from template updates.
suggested_adr_content.decision: Move AC#5 implementation to STORY-091 or dedicated story-creation enhancement story
rationale: Template updates (AC#1-4) are configuration/file changes - can complete independently
AC#5 requires skill code modification and Phase 1 workflow testing - separate story appropriate
Parallel development: template can be released independently while skill enhancement is in development
Prevents scope creep in already-complex story

analysis: STORY-090 defers AC#5 to 'STORY-091 or future story-creation enhancement'
STORY-091 frontmatter: No deferrals detected - all ACs pending implementation
STORY-091 does NOT defer work back to STORY-090
Dependency direction is ONE-WAY: STORY-090 (prerequisite) → STORY-091 (dependent)

multi_level_chain_detection.rca_007_applicability: Yes - RCA-007 addresses multi-level deferral chains

chain_details.action: Implements Feature 1 (Git Worktrees) - does not include AC#5 work
Implements Feature 2 (Test Isolation) - does not include AC#5 work

rca_007_applicability_analysis: RCA-007 concerned about STORY-004 → STORY-005 → STORY-006 chain losing work
Current chain (STORY-090 → 091 → 092) is DIFFERENT: work flows linearly within EPIC-010
AC#5 is NOT deferred to STORY-091 - deferred to 'future story-creation enhancement'
STORY-091 focuses on Git worktrees, STORY-092 on test isolation - orthogonal to AC#5
AC#5 belongs to story-creation skill enhancements (separate concern from parallel development)
Risk: Low - AC#5 is explicitly called out as deferred to separate story (not lost in chain)
multi_level_chain_detection.risk_assessment: ACCEPTABLE - work not lost, but requires explicit follow-up story creation
multi_level_chain_detection.mitigation_required: Create follow-up story for AC#5 implementation (story-creation skill enhancement) to prevent work from being lost in EPIC-010 chain
multi_level_chain_detection.recommendation: Add dependency tracking: Create STORY-093 (or similar) for story-creation skill AC#5 enhancement, document link to STORY-090 deferral

story_includes_deferred_work_analysis: STORY-090 defers: AC#5 (skill Phase 1 dependency question + input normalization)
STORY-091 scope: Automatic worktree creation, idle detection, cleanup prompts (7 ACs)
Keywords searched in STORY-091: 'dependency question', 'depends_on field', 'story-creation skill', 'Phase 1', 'normalization'
Result: NO MATCHES - STORY-091 does not include AC#5 work
STORY-091 is prerequisites ONLY - provides dependency field, but does not implement skill enhancement
referenced_story_validation.missing_deferred_work_issue: STORY-091 is dependency-tracked but does NOT include deferred AC#5 work
missing_deferred_work_remediation: AC#5 work should be in a different story (story-creation skill enhancement)
STORY-091 deferral reference ('STORY-091 or future story-creation enhancement') is intentional
Follow-up: Create dedicated story-creation skill enhancement story (STORY-093+)

overall_deferral_justification.primary_justification: Skill enhancement (AC#5) properly scoped separately from template updates (AC#1-4)
completeness_of_current_work: 5 of 7 ACs fully implemented and tested
Template file updated with depends_on field
6 existing stories standardized to array format
overall_deferral_justification.standalone_value: HIGH - Provides depends_on infrastructure for EPIC-010 parallel development feature 3
overall_deferral_justification.deferral_blockers: None - deferrals are appropriately scoped, not blockers

violations.description: Deferring AC#5 (in original DoD) requires ADR documentation
items_affected: AC#5: Story-Creation Skill Phase 1 Dependency Question
violations.rationale: AC#5 was in Definition of Done (in scope). Moving to future story is scope change. Per critical-rules.md, scope changes require ADR.
violations.evidence: Story DoD line 440-441 marks AC#5 items as [ ] (deferred). DoD status section explains deferral rationale but no ADR created.
violations.remediation: Create ADR-008 (or next available) documenting scope boundary between template updates and skill enhancement. Reference in Implementation Notes.
violations.user_approval_path: If user approves deferral without ADR via AskUserQuestion during QA, mark as 'User Approved Deferral' in story
violations.impact_if_unfixed: Story cannot be QA Approved without either: (1) ADR creation, or (2) explicit user approval via AskUserQuestion

violations.description: AC#5 deferred to 'future story' but follow-up story not yet created
items_affected: AC#5: Story-Creation Skill Phase 1 Dependency Question
violations.rationale: Deferral references 'STORY-091 or future story-creation enhancement' - unclear which follow-up will implement AC#5
violations.evidence: Story Implementation Notes line 543: 'Deferred to follow-up story (STORY-091 or future story-creation enhancement)'
violations.risk: RCA-007 risk: AC#5 work could be lost if follow-up story is never created
violations.remediation: Create STORY-093 (or next available) for story-creation skill enhancement. Document depends_on: ['STORY-090']. Include AC#5 work in acceptance criteria.
violations.user_approval_path: Include follow-up story in QA approval: 'User approved deferral with commitment to create STORY-093 for skill enhancement'
violations.impact_if_unfixed: AC#5 work not implemented in release - reduced feature scope for story-creation skill

summary.qa_approval_recommendation: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL - User must address 2 medium violations
approval_conditions: CONDITION 1: Create ADR-008 documenting scope boundary (template updates vs skill enhancement) and reference in Implementation Notes
CONDITION 2: Commit to creating STORY-093 (or next available) for story-creation skill enhancement with AC#5 work, OR user approval via AskUserQuestion accepting deferral risk

immediate_actions.description: Document scope boundary decision: why AC#5 (skill enhancement) deferred from AC#1-4 (template updates)
Add reference to ADR-008 in Implementation Notes (lines 546-547) or document explicit user approval
immediate_actions.deliverable: Story file updated with ADR reference or AskUserQuestion approval record

follow_up_actions.description: Story-Creation Skill Enhancement: Implement AC#5 (Phase 1 dependency question + input normalization) with integration testing
follow_up_actions.deliverable: New story with depends_on: ['STORY-090'], includes AC#5 acceptance criteria

chain_analysis_detailed.chain_safety_assessment: SAFE - No circular deferrals, no multi-level deferral chains within EPIC-010 critical path. AC#5 work is orthogonal to worktrees/test isolation.
chain_analysis_detailed.rca_007_risk_level: LOW - RCA-007 risk mitigated by explicit 'future story' reference, but requires follow-up story creation to prevent work loss

recommendations.recommendation: Do not approve STORY-090 for QA release without ADR-008 or explicit user approval
recommendations.rationale: Scope changes (AC#5 deferral) require documentation per critical-rules.md rule #9
recommendations.implementation: Require ADR creation or AskUserQuestion approval before QA transition

recommendations.recommendation: Create follow-up story for AC#5 immediately after STORY-090 release
recommendations.rationale: Prevents RCA-007 risk (work lost in story chain)
recommendations.implementation: Create STORY-093 with depends_on: ['STORY-090'], schedule for SPRINT-6

recommendations.recommendation: Link follow-up story in STORY-090 Implementation Notes
recommendations.implementation: Update Implementation Notes: 'Follow-up story: STORY-093 (story-creation skill enhancement)'

recommendations.recommendation: Update deferral reference in Implementation Notes
recommendations.rationale: Currently vague ('STORY-091 or future story-creation enhancement') - should specify story ID after follow-up created
recommendations.implementation: Change line 543 from 'STORY-091 or future...' to specific story ID once created

validation_conclusion.overall_assessment: DEFERRED ITEMS ARE JUSTIFIED WITH APPROPRIATE SCOPE BOUNDARY
validation_conclusion.technical_soundness: SOUND - Skill enhancement properly deferred from template updates
validation_conclusion.governance_compliance: NON-COMPLIANT - Missing ADR for scope change
validation_conclusion.risk_level: MEDIUM - RCA-007 risk mitigated if follow-up story created promptly
validation_conclusion.approval_ready: NO - Requires ADR-008 or user approval before QA transition
next_steps: 1. Create ADR-008 (or obtain user approval via AskUserQuestion)
2. Update Implementation Notes with ADR reference
3. Create STORY-093 for skill enhancement follow-up
